title: Runner Skeleton: run --issue, Host-Repo Resolution, Collision Check
summary: Deterministic runner framework for golemic run --issue N. Resolves the host repo, loads config and credentials, generates a runId, creates the event log, writes run_started, loads the issue via gh, and performs a collision check (worktree, branch, or open PR already exists) before any worktree is created.

stakeholder_intent.actor: Human maintainer (or future run.sh polling loop)
stakeholder_intent.goal: Start a deterministic run for a given issue number, ensuring no conflicting state exists
stakeholder_intent.business_value: Provides the safe entry point for the entire loop: validates preconditions, detects conflicts early, and records the run start in the event log
stakeholder_intent.trigger: golemic run --issue 42 is invoked from the host repo root
stakeholder_intent.success_outcome: A run is started with runId, run_started event is written, and no collision exists; the runner is ready to proceed to worktree creation

in_scope: Host-Repo resolution: git root from cwd; if under tools/golemic, resolve the surrounding repo
origin remote and default branch (main) resolution
Event log creation and run_started event
Issue loading via gh issue view N --json title,body with dev token
Collision check: existing worktree under ~/.golemic/<project>/worktrees/issue-<N>, local/remote branch golemic/issue-<N>, or open PR with that head branch
Collision → abort with clear message including concrete cleanup commands, outcome aborted
Fail-closed: missing config/credentials abort before any GitHub access
out_of_scope: Loop orchestration beyond the initial check (I1.10)

preconditions: Config loader (I0.2) and credentials loader (I0.3) are implemented

business_rules.rule: Host repo is determined by walking up from cwd; if cwd contains 'tools/golemic' in its path, resolve to the enclosing git root
business_rules.outcome: repoRoot is set to the host repo's git root

business_rules.rule: Config and credentials must load successfully before any GitHub or git operation
business_rules.outcome: Load failure → clear error, exit != 0, no run_started event

business_rules.rule: runId format is issue-<N>-<timestamp> where timestamp is a compact, sortable format
business_rules.outcome: runId is set to e.g. issue-42-20260715T142530Z

business_rules.rule: Collision: if a worktree ~/.golemic/<project>/worktrees/issue-<N> exists → abort
business_rules.outcome: Error message: 'Worktree exists at <path>; remove with: git worktree remove <path>', outcome aborted

business_rules.rule: Collision: if a local or remote branch golemic/issue-<N> exists → abort
business_rules.outcome: Error message: 'Branch golemic/issue-<N> exists locally or on origin; remove with: git branch -D golemic/issue-<N> && git push origin --delete golemic/issue-<N>', outcome aborted

business_rules.rule: Collision: if an open PR exists with head branch golemic/issue-<N> → abort
business_rules.outcome: Error message: 'Open PR <url> exists with head branch golemic/issue-<N>; close it first', outcome aborted

business_rules.rule: The run_started event must be the first event in the log and must be written before any collision check that involves GitHub
business_rules.outcome: run_started is written with issue and runId payload; if collision check fails, the log still contains run_started with outcome aborted

decision_tables.name: Collision check decision
inputs: Open PR with head branch exists
decision_tables.default_outcome: Abort: collision state is not clear

outputs.status: exit 0 (preliminary; actual exit depends on I1.10 full outcome)
outputs.body: runId printed to stdout, run_started event written

errors.message: Worktree exists at <path>; remove with: git worktree remove <path>

errors.condition: Open PR with head branch exists
errors.message: Open PR <url> exists with head branch golemic/issue-<N>; close it first

process_steps.name: Resolve host repo
process_steps.action: Determine git root from cwd or enclosing repo if under tools/golemic
process_steps.state_after: repoRoot, origin remote URL, default branch name are known
process_steps.failure_behavior: Exit with error: 'not in a git repository'

process_steps.name: Load config and credentials
process_steps.action: Load .golemic/config.json via config.Load, then load credentials via credentials.NewLoader
process_steps.state_after: Config and credentials are loaded and validated
process_steps.failure_behavior: Exit with clear error naming the failure (config or credentials), no GitHub access attempted

process_steps.name: Generate runId and create event log
process_steps.action: Generate runId = issue-<N>-<timestamp>, create event log directory, write run_started event
process_steps.state_after: runId assigned, events.jsonl created with run_started as first event
process_steps.failure_behavior: Exit with error: 'failed to create event log'

process_steps.name: Load issue from GitHub
process_steps.action: Run gh issue view N --json title,body with dev token, parse response
process_steps.state_before: Issue details are not yet loaded
process_steps.state_after: Issue title, body, and number are known
process_steps.failure_behavior: Exit with error: 'failed to load issue N: <gh error>', outcome mapped to dev_failed in I1.10

process_steps.name: Collision check
process_steps.action: Check for existing worktree, local/remote branch golemic/issue-<N>, and open PR with that head branch
process_steps.outcome: No collision → proceed to worktree creation. Collision → abort with cleanup commands.
process_steps.failure_behavior: On any collision: write run_finished with outcome aborted, exit != 0 with concrete cleanup commands

integration_contracts.transport: gh issue view N --json title,body via Executor.RunWithEnv with GH_TOKEN=dev_token
integration_contracts.failure_mapping: Non-zero exit → 'failed to load issue N'

integration_contracts.operation: Check for existing worktree and local branch
integration_contracts.request_contract: git worktree list, git branch --list golemic/issue-<N>, git ls-remote --heads origin golemic/issue-<N>
integration_contracts.failure_mapping: Non-zero exit → 'failed to check git state'

security.authentication: Dev token (GH_TOKEN) is used for gh issue view; token is loaded from credentials (I0.3)
authorization_rules: The dev token must have read access to the host repo's issues
audit_requirements: run_started event records the run start with issue number and runId

architecture_constraints: Config and credentials must be loaded before any GitHub access
All external commands go through the injectable Executor interface
run_started must be the first event in the log
allowed_changes: Add run subcommand handler to main.go
Create internal/runner package for run orchestration
forbidden_changes: Do not access GitHub before config/credentials are loaded
implementation_context.consistency_and_concurrency: Single-threaded CLI; no concurrency concerns. The collision check is inherently racy (concurrent runs), but the deterministic branch name makes the second run fail with a clear collision message.

acceptance_scenarios.title: Happy path: run starts with no collision
given: No worktree, branch, or PR exists for issue-42
then: run_started event is written with issue=42 and a valid runId
Exit code is 0 (preliminary; actual outcome determined by I1.10)

acceptance_scenarios.title: Worktree collision aborts with cleanup commands
then: Error message includes 'Worktree exists at' and the git worktree remove command

acceptance_scenarios.title: Branch collision aborts with cleanup commands
given: Branch golemic/issue-42 exists locally or remotely
then: Error message includes branch name and git branch deletion commands

acceptance_scenarios.title: Open PR collision aborts with PR URL
given: An open PR exists with head branch golemic/issue-42
then: Error message includes the PR URL and instructions to close it

acceptance_scenarios.title: Missing config causes clear error before any GitHub access
then: Error message mentions config file not found
No gh or git command was executed (beyond repo root detection)

test_cases: Happy path writes run_started with correct payload
Worktree collision aborts with cleanup message
Missing config aborts before GitHub access
Missing credentials aborts before GitHub access
non_functional_requirements: All external commands go through injectable Executor for testability
Error messages include concrete cleanup commands
definition_of_done: go build ./... && go vet ./... && go test -count=1 ./... is green
Run subcommand is wired into main.go
Collision checks cover all three cases (worktree, branch, PR)

decision_log.decision: If cwd is under tools/golemic, resolve the enclosing git repo as the host repo
decision_log.rationale: Specified in §2.7: golemic is a tool dropped into tools/golemic and works on the enclosing repo

decision_log.decision: Check worktree, then local branch, then remote branch, then open PR; report first collision found
decision_log.rationale: Specified in backlog: 'existiert Worktree ... lokaler oder Remote-Branch ... oder offener PR'

decision_log.decision: Runner uses the same Executor interface and config/credentials packages as preflight
decision_log.rationale: internal/preflight defines the Executor interface; internal/config and internal/credentials provide loading and validation

decision_log.decision: Runner uses the existing Executor interface for all gh and git commands
decision_log.rationale: main.go osExecutor already implements the Executor interface; runner will reuse it

codebase_evidence.finding: Executor interface with Run and RunWithEnv methods exists; all external commands are injectable
Config.Load exists and validates required fields with clear error messages
Credentials loader with injectable homeDir exists; env var precedence over file is implemented
run is already listed as a known command with description 'Run the main process (not implemented)' and dispatches to 'not implemented' path
